title: hieracium wiesbaurianum subsp. lithophilum
intro: Hieracium wiesbaurianum subsp. lithophilum, also known as Rock Hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. As a medicinal plant, Rock Hawkweed is rich in bioactive compounds and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, offering potential health benefits such as immune system support, wound healing, and antiseptic properties. Rock Hawkweed is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion for use in herbal medicine, although its availability and use may be limited due to conservation concerns.
